conjecture_title: Open Problem: $\Delta + O(1)$ bound for neighbour sum distinguishing colourings
paper_title: Progress on the adjacent vertex distinguishing edge colouring conjecture
summary: The open problem asks whether the number of colours for a neighbour sum distinguishing edge colouring can be bounded by $\Delta + O(1)$. The best known asymptotic upper bound remains $\Delta + O(\Delta^{1/2})$, due to Przyby\l{}o (arXiv:1703.00406, Random Structures & Algorithms 2015/2017). The stronger Flandrin et al.\ conjecture that $\Delta + 2$ colours suffice also remains open. No paper found after 2020 closes the gap between $\Delta + O(\Delta^{1/2})$ and $\Delta + O(1)$.
search_queries: neighbour sum distinguishing edge colouring Delta O(1) bound Przybylo
neighbour sum distinguishing edge colouring conjecture Flandrin arxiv 2021 2022 2023 2024
neighbour sum distinguishing index Delta constant bound proof 2023 2024 2025 arxiv
notes: No follow-up resolving the Delta + O(1) open problem was found. The best known bound Delta + O(Delta^{1/2}) due to Przybylo (arXiv:1703.00406) already predates the 2020 source paper. A 2025 paper (arXiv:2511.01835) studies a quasi-majority variant of neighbour sum distinguishing edge-colourings but does not address the classical Delta + O(1) open problem.
